meta_title: For Students: content creation to Solve can't debug code (Advanced Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to content creation and overcome can't debug code. A Advanced-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Debug Like a Pro: Content Creation Strategies for AI Builders
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with bugs. Learn how to leverage content creation and advanced tools like Antigravity and Claude Code to master your development workflow.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't just fixing code; it's a documentation challenge. By shifting to a 'content-first' development mindset, you externalize your internal logic. Use Antigravity to map your architecture and Claude Code to perform iterative, context-aware bug squashing. This approach turns your hardest errors into high-value technical documentation for your portfolio.
content.intro:
You are staring at a terminal full of red text, the cursor blinking in rhythmic mockery of your progress. You’ve spent hours in the trenches, yet the bug remains, an invisible wall between you and your release. You aren't just stuck; you are currently suffering from a lack of technical visibility that plagues every developer from junior to senior.

But here is the secret the pros won't tell you: debugging is not a solo battle of wits. It is a process of content creation. When you document your logic and externalize your stack's state, you gain the clarity needed to crush errors. By integrating Antigravity and Claude Code into your workflow, you transform your debugging process from a frustrating chore into a structured, portfolio-building asset.

steps.title: Externalize the State
steps.description: Before fixing, you must document the current state. Create a 'bug-log' file that details the expected output versus reality.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current codebase state and generate a structured 'bug-log' markdown file that outlines the discrepancy between the expected and actual behavior.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create a debug-summary.md file capturing the current state and error logs.'

steps.title: Architecture Mapping
steps.description: Use Antigravity to visualize the flow of data. If you can't visualize the data path, you can't find the break.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a visual flow diagram of the current authentication module to identify where the data packet is dropping.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate an architecture map for the current module to identify data flow bottlenecks.'

steps.title: Iterative Hypothesizing
steps.description: Treat every bug fix as a hypothesis. Use Claude Code to test these hypotheses systematically to avoid 'shotgun debugging'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Propose three potential root causes for this specific error and rank them by probability based on the stack trace provided.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Run unit tests for each proposed root cause and report the failure rate.'

steps.title: Automated Refactoring
steps.description: Once the bug is identified, use Claude Code to implement a fix that aligns with your project's existing design patterns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the identified faulty function to adhere to clean code principles while fixing the memory leak.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Apply the fix to the identified function and verify with existing tests.'

steps.title: Post-Mortem Documentation
steps.description: The final step of debugging is writing about it. Turn your resolved bug into a 'Lessons Learned' post for your portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a technical blog post explaining the bug, the methodology used to solve it, and the final solution.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate a technical post-mortem report based on the debug-summary.md file.'

pro_tips: Always commit your 'failed' attempts to a separate branch; it builds a rich history of your problem-solving process for recruiters.
Use Claude Code to generate 'rubber duck' explanations of your code to see if you can identify the bug while explaining it aloud.
Treat your error logs as data; use Antigravity to identify patterns in your bugs over a 30-day sprint.
Never fix a bug without writing a corresponding test case; it prevents regression and adds value to your repository.
Maintain a 'Bug-to-Feature' pipeline where every resolved bug is documented as an upgrade to your system's stability.
content.next_steps: Once you have successfully resolved your current bug, apply this same 'Content Creation' framework to your next feature rollout to ensure high-quality, documented code from day one.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to turn every error into an opportunity for growth and documentation. Stop fearing the terminal and start building with confidence. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
